root_text.an1.394:1.1: “Accharāsaṅghātamattampi ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ bhāveti, ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave:
root_text.an1.394:1.2: ‘bhikkhu arittajjhāno viharati, satthusāsanakaro ovādapatikaro, amoghaṁ raṭṭhapiṇḍaṁ bhuñjati’.
root_text.an1.394:1.3: Ko pana vādo ye naṁ bahulīkarontī”ti.
root_text.an1.395:1.1: “Accharāsaṅghātamattampi ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ bhāveti …pe…
root_text.an1.402:1.1: Kāye kāyānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ …pe…
root_text.an1.405:1.1: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.an1.406:1.1: Anuppannānaṁ pāpakānaṁ akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ anuppādāya chandaṁ janeti vāyamati vīriyaṁ ārabhati cittaṁ paggaṇhāti padahati;
root_text.an1.407:1.1: uppannānaṁ pāpakānaṁ akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ pahānāya chandaṁ janeti vāyamati vīriyaṁ ārabhati cittaṁ paggaṇhāti padahati.
root_text.an1.408:1.1: Anuppannānaṁ kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ uppādāya chandaṁ janeti vāyamati vīriyaṁ ārabhati cittaṁ paggaṇhāti padahati;
root_text.an1.409:1.1: uppannānaṁ kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ ṭhitiyā asammosāya bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya bhāvanāya pāripūriyā chandaṁ janeti vāyamati vīriyaṁ ārabhati cittaṁ paggaṇhāti padahati.
root_text.an1.439:1.1: Ajjhattaṁ rūpasaññī bahiddhā rūpāni passati parittāni suvaṇṇadubbaṇṇāni.
root_text.an1.440:1.1: … ajjhattaṁ rūpasaññī bahiddhā rūpāni passati appamāṇāni suvaṇṇadubbaṇṇāni.
root_text.an1.441:1.1: … ajjhattaṁ arūpasaññī bahiddhā rūpāni passati parittāni suvaṇṇadubbaṇṇāni.
root_text.an1.442:1.1: … ajjhattaṁ arūpasaññī bahiddhā rūpāni passati appamāṇāni suvaṇṇadubbaṇṇāni.
root_text.an1.443:1.1: … ajjhattaṁ arūpasaññī bahiddhā rūpāni passati nīlāni nīlavaṇṇāni nīlanidassanāni nīlanibhāsāni.
root_text.an1.444:1.1: … ajjhattaṁ arūpasaññī bahiddhā rūpāni passati pītāni pītavaṇṇāni pītanidassanāni pītanibhāsāni.
root_text.an1.445:1.1: … ajjhattaṁ arūpasaññī bahiddhā rūpāni passati lohitakāni lohitakavaṇṇāni lohitakanidassanāni lohitakanibhāsāni.
root_text.an1.446:1.1: … ajjhattaṁ arūpasaññī bahiddhā rūpāni passati odātāni odātavaṇṇāni odātanidassanāni odātanibhāsāni.
root_text.an1.450:1.1: sabbaso rūpasaññānaṁ samatikkamā paṭighasaññānaṁ atthaṅgamā nānattasaññānaṁ amanasikārā ananto ākāsoti ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati …
root_text.an1.451:1.1: sabbaso ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ samatikkamma anantaṁ viññāṇanti viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati …
root_text.an1.452:1.1: sabbaso viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ samatikkamma natthi kiñcīti ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati …
root_text.an1.453:1.1: sabbaso ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ samatikkamma nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati …
root_text.an1.454:1.1: sabbaso nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ samatikkamma saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ upasampajja viharati ….
root_text.an1.574:2.2: ‘bhikkhu arittajjhāno viharati satthusāsanakaro ovādapatikaro, amoghaṁ raṭṭhapiṇḍaṁ bhuñjati’.
root_text.an1.574:2.3: Ko pana vādo ye naṁ bahulīkarontī”ti.

translation_text.an1.394:1.1: “If, mendicants, a mendicant develops the first absorption, even as long as a finger-snap, they are called
translation_text.an1.394:1.2: a mendicant who does not lack absorption, who follows the Teacher’s instructions, who responds to advice, and who does not eat the country’s alms in vain.
translation_text.an1.394:1.3: How much more so those who make much of it!”
translation_text.an1.395:1.1: “If, mendicants, a mendicant develops the second …
translation_text.an1.398:1.1: or the heart’s release by love …
translation_text.an1.399:1.1: or the heart’s release by compassion …
translation_text.an1.400:1.1: or the heart’s release by rejoicing …
translation_text.an1.401:1.1: or the heart’s release by equanimity, even as long as a finger-snap …
translation_text.an1.402:1.1: If a mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of the body …
translation_text.an1.405:1.1: principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world, even for the time of a finger-snap …
translation_text.an1.406:1.1: If they generate enthusiasm, try, make an effort, exert the mind, and strive so that bad, unskillful qualities don’t arise, even for the time of a finger-snap …
translation_text.an1.407:1.1: If they generate enthusiasm, try, make an effort, exert the mind, and strive so that bad, unskillful qualities that have arisen are given up, even for the time of a finger-snap …
translation_text.an1.408:1.1: If they generate enthusiasm, try, make an effort, exert the mind, and strive so that skillful qualities that have not arisen do arise, even for the time of a finger-snap …
translation_text.an1.409:1.1: If they generate enthusiasm, try, make an effort, exert the mind, and strive so that skillful qualities that have arisen remain, are not lost, but increase, mature, and are fulfilled by development, even for the time of a finger-snap …
translation_text.an1.410:1.1: If they develop the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to enthusiasm, and active effort …
translation_text.an1.411:1.1: the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to energy, and active effort …
translation_text.an1.412:1.1: the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to mental development, and active effort …
translation_text.an1.413:1.1: the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to inquiry, and active effort, even for the time of a finger-snap …
translation_text.an1.414:1.1: If they develop the faculty of faith …
translation_text.an1.418:1.1: the faculty of wisdom, even for the time of a finger-snap …
translation_text.an1.419:1.1: If they develop the power of faith …
translation_text.an1.423:1.1: the power of wisdom, even for the time of a finger-snap …
translation_text.an1.424:1.1: If they develop the awakening factor of mindfulness …
translation_text.an1.425:1.1: the awakening factor of investigation of principles …
translation_text.an1.430:1.1: the awakening factor of equanimity, even for the time of a finger-snap …
translation_text.an1.438:1.1: right immersion, even for the time of a finger-snap …
translation_text.an1.439:1.1: Perceiving form internally, they see forms externally, limited, both pretty and ugly.
translation_text.an1.439:1.2: Mastering them, they are aware that:
translation_text.an1.440:1.1: Perceiving form internally, they see forms externally, limitless, both pretty and ugly.
translation_text.an1.440:1.2: Mastering them, they are aware that:
translation_text.an1.441:1.1: Not perceiving form internally, they see forms externally, limited, both pretty and ugly.
translation_text.an1.441:1.2: Mastering them, they are aware that:
translation_text.an1.442:1.1: Not perceiving form internally, they see forms externally, limitless, both pretty and ugly.
translation_text.an1.442:1.2: Mastering them, they are aware that:
translation_text.an1.443:1.1: Not perceiving form internally, they see forms externally that are blue, with blue color and blue appearance.
translation_text.an1.443:1.2: Mastering them, they are aware that:
translation_text.an1.444:1.1: Not perceiving form internally, they see forms externally that are yellow, with yellow color and yellow appearance.
translation_text.an1.444:1.2: Mastering them, they are aware that:
translation_text.an1.445:1.1: Not perceiving form internally, they see forms externally that are red, with red color and red appearance.
translation_text.an1.445:1.2: Mastering them, they are aware that:
translation_text.an1.446:1.1: Not perceiving form internally, they see forms externally that are white, with white color and white appearance.
translation_text.an1.446:1.2: Mastering them, they are aware that:
translation_text.an1.447:1.1: Having physical form, they see forms …
translation_text.an1.448:1.1: not perceiving form internally, they see forms externally …
translation_text.an1.450:1.1: going totally beyond perceptions of form, with the disappearance of perceptions of impingement, not focusing on perceptions of diversity, aware that ‘space is infinite’, they enter and remain in the dimension of infinite space …
translation_text.an1.451:1.1: going totally beyond the dimension of infinite space, aware that ‘consciousness is infinite’, they enter and remain in the dimension of infinite consciousness …
translation_text.an1.452:1.1: going totally beyond the dimension of infinite consciousness, aware that ‘there is nothing at all’, they enter and remain in the dimension of nothingness …
translation_text.an1.453:1.1: going totally beyond the dimension of nothingness, they enter and remain in the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception …
translation_text.an1.454:1.1: going totally beyond the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception, they enter and remain in the cessation of perception and feeling …
translation_text.an1.455:1.1: They develop the meditation on universal earth …
translation_text.an1.465:1.1: They develop the perception of ugliness …
translation_text.an1.467:1.1: the perception of the repulsiveness of food …
translation_text.an1.468:1.1: the perception of dissatisfaction with the whole world …
translation_text.an1.470:1.1: the perception of suffering in impermanence …
translation_text.an1.471:1.1: the perception of not-self in suffering …
translation_text.an1.475:1.1: They develop the perception of impermanence …
translation_text.an1.478:1.1: the perception of the repulsiveness of food …
translation_text.an1.479:1.1: the perception of dissatisfaction with the whole world …
translation_text.an1.481:1.1: the perception of the worm-infested corpse …
translation_text.an1.482:1.1: the perception of the livid corpse …
translation_text.an1.483:1.1: the perception of the split open corpse …
translation_text.an1.484:1.1: the perception of the bloated corpse …
translation_text.an1.485:1.1: They develop the recollection of the Buddha …
translation_text.an1.495:1.1: They develop the faculty of faith together with the first absorption …
translation_text.an1.504:1.1: the power of wisdom together with the first absorption …
translation_text.an1.565:1.1: They develop the faculty of faith together with equanimity …
translation_text.an1.566:1.1: They develop the faculty of energy …
translation_text.an1.574:2.2: a mendicant who does not lack absorption, who follows the Teacher’s instructions, who responds to advice, and who does not eat the country’s alms in vain.
translation_text.an1.574:2.3: How much more so those who make much of it!”
